claimText: The Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) framework applies to both banks and Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs).
evidenceExcerpt: Applicability: It applies to both banks and NBFCs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope and applicability of the PCA framework.
distractorHooks: Applies only to Scheduled Commercial Banks
Applies only to public sector banks

claimText: For Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs), the Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) framework replaced the Supervisory Action Framework (SAF).
evidenceExcerpt: PCA framework for UCBs replaced the existing Supervisory Action Framework (SAF).
examUse: Statement-based question on the regulatory transition for Urban Co-operative Banks.
distractorHooks: Replaced the Liquidity Coverage Ratio framework
SAF replaced the PCA framework for UCBs

claimText: The key areas monitored under the Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) framework are Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR), Asset Quality, and Profitability.
evidenceExcerpt: Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR), Asset Quality and Profitability are the key areas for monitoring.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying the specific parameters used by RBI to trigger PCA.

claimText: Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR), also known as Capital to Risk-weighted Asset Ratio (CRAR), is the ratio of total capital to total risk-weighted assets expressed in percentage terms.
evidenceExcerpt: CAR is the ratio of total capital to total risk-weighted assets in percentage terms. It is also called Capital to Risk-weighted Asset Ratio (CRAR).
examUse: Concept-based question on the definition and formula of Capital Adequacy Ratio.
distractorHooks: Ratio of total assets to total liabilities
Ratio of risk-weighted assets to total deposits
Ratio of tier 1 capital to total non-performing assets
